title: Díaz hits leadoff homer and Aranda delivers go-ahead single to lift Rays over Red Sox 3-1
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Boston Red Sox Aside from Marcelo Mayer’s home run, only one Red Sox player reached third base.
Tampa Bay Rays' Yandy Díaz celebrates his solo home run off Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early during the first inning.
AP Photo/Chris O'Meara ST.

title: MBTA announces service changes, extended hours for World Cup
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: World Cup Most Commuter Rail lines will operate on reduced schedules.
The walkway connecting the Foxborough commuter rail station to Gillette Stadium.
Danielle Parhizkaran/The Boston Globe As Greater Boston readies itself for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, the MBTA is implementing temporary service adjustments for the upcoming summer.

title: Red Sox reportedly not considering firing Craig Breslow
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox "The club’s ownership hierarchy has not even discussed making a change, the source said.
” Craig Breslow fired Alex Cora and several coaches earlier this season.
Barry Chin/The Boston Globe The 2026 Boston Red Sox have left a lot to be desired so far this season.

title: Patrice Bergeron likes what he sees from young Bruins center Fraser Minten
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins "I was really impressed with how he wanted to learn and improve, ask questions, and he was really taking everything in.
" Fraser Minten scored 17 goals in his first full season with the Bruins.
AP Photo/Nick Wass ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Expect some ‘toasty’ days ahead, say meteorologists
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News “Wed-late week: Heat and humidity build mid to late-week with showers/storms possible," the National Weather Service posted.
Relief from the summer heat in the Frog Pond on the Boston Common.
Matthew J.

title: Hampshire College has secured a loan to offer classes through the fall semester, school says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News Students entering their final semester will now be able to receive their degrees, as planned.
People walk across campus at Hampshire College in Amherst.

Finn Gomez/The Boston Globe Hampshire College says it has secured financing that will allow it to complete a fall 2026 semester before closing for good, reversing concerns raised last week that the school might not have enough money to carry out the process.

title: Payton Pritchard says people are taking Jaylen Brown’s ‘favorite year’ comments the wrong way
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics Pritchard told reporters he thinks Brown was expressing pride in a successful regular-season.
Jaylen Brown playing for the Celtics during 2025-26 season.
AP Photo/Charles Krupa Jaylen Brown has said, on multiple occasions, that this past season was the one he liked the most of his decade-long NBA career.

title: A 2025 NFL re-draft has Patriots choosing another offensive lineman with 4th overall pick
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots A new re-draft has the Patriots choosing to take another left tackle over Will Campbell.
Will Campbell had a rocky end to his rookie year with the Patriots.

(Danielle Parhizkaran/Globe Staff) Just days after the Patriots’ 2025 season came to an end in Super Bowl LX, Mike Vrabel vouched for rookie left tackle Will Campbell and his long-term future in Foxborough.

title: Alex Cora still helping out Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox “We are a fraternity, I learned that at a young age, and you have to pay it forward.
" Alex Cora was fired by the Red Sox in late April amid a 10-17 start.

AP Photo/Jim Davis It’s been more than a month since the Red Sox made the decision to fire longtime manager Alex Cora and several members of his coaching staff amid a sluggish start to the 2026 season.

title: AirBnB donating more than 1,100 free World Cup tickets for Mass. youth, Healey announces
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: World Cup “These tickets will help make sure that kids who otherwise may never have the opportunity to attend a match can experience the excitement of the world's biggest sporting event,” Healey said.
More than one thousand Massachusetts children will be able to attend World Cup games for free through tickets donated by AirBnB, officials announced.

AirBnB is donating 1,104 World Cup tickets to the state’s Boys & Girls Clubs, the Department of Children and Families (DCF), and community groups in the state, the office of Governor Maura Healey said in a press release Monday.
